perturbation.original_text: mPhase agrees to abide by the guidelines appearing on Schedule B of this Agreement specifying the dimensions, requirements and specifications, and the review process related to use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo. mPhase understands that these guidelines may be updated from time to time and agrees to abide by those updates as well as the guidelines provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: mPhase agrees to abide by the guidelines appearing on Schedule B of this Agreement specifying the dimensions, requirements and specifications, and the review process related to use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo. mPhase understands that these guidelines may be updated from time to time, but is not obligated to abide by those updates unless mutually agreed upon in writing.
perturbation.explanation: By changing mPhase's obligation to abide by updated guidelines from mandatory to optional with written agreement, it contradicts the initial binding agreement to follow Schedule B specifications. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the enforceability of guideline updates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: mPhase agrees to allow Lucent to inspect the Approved Uses bearing the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, to ensure that those uses reviewed and approved pursuant to the procedure set forth in Schedule B continue to be in compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that mPhase agrees to abide by updated guidelines. The changed text modifies this obligation, stating that mPhase is 'not obligated to abide by those updates unless mutually agreed upon in writing.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that mPhase agrees to allow Lucent to inspect the Approved Uses...to ensure that those uses reviewed and approved pursuant to the procedure set forth in Schedule B continue to be in compliance with the terms of this Agreement'. If mPhase is not obligated to abide by updates to Schedule B unless mutually agreed upon, Lucent cannot ensure compliance with the 'terms of this Agreement' based on Schedule B, leading to an in-text contradiction. This falls under the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction perturbation type as the enforceability of guideline updates becomes ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: Lucent is generally aware of the current quality of mPhase's Goods. mPhase agrees to maintain the quality of its Goods associated with the Lucent Co-Branding Logo to a level of quality comparable to the current quality of their Goods.
perturbation.changed_text: Lucent is generally aware of the current quality of mPhase's Goods. mPhase agrees to use its best efforts to maintain the quality of its Goods associated with the Lucent Co-Branding Logo to a level of quality generally acceptable to the industry.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies maintaining quality comparable to the current quality of their goods, a tangible metric. By changing it to 'best efforts' to achieve a 'generally acceptable' standard introduces ambiguity. This is because 'best efforts' is subjective and 'generally acceptable to the industry' is vague, contradicting the initial clause about quality control.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If Lucent determines that mPhase's Goods are no longer maintained at the current level of quality, Lucent shall so notify mPhase, in writing, and Lucent shall have the right to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines quality based on a tangible reference point (current quality), which allows Lucent to determine if 'mPhase's Goods are no longer maintained at the current level of quality.' The changed text shifts the standard to 'best efforts' and 'generally acceptable to the industry,' introducing ambiguity and subjectivity. This directly contradicts Lucent's right to terminate the agreement if the goods are not maintained at the 'current level of quality,' because that level is no longer clearly defined. It is now based on a subjective standard that is ambiguous and open to interpretation. This creates a direct conflict, because the condition that triggers Lucent's right to terminate the agreement is now unclear. This fits the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, mPhase shall immediately cease use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, provided, however, that mPhase shall have no obligation to remove such Co-Branding Logo from any Goods sold prior to the date of such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, mPhase shall immediately cease use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, and shall make commercially reasonable efforts to remove such Co-Branding Logo from any Goods sold prior to the date of such termination.
perturbation.explanation: By introducing a requirement for mPhase to make 'commercially reasonable efforts' to remove the Co-Branding Logo from goods sold before termination, it creates an obligation where previously there was none. This obligation can be interpreted in many ways. This creates an ambiguity in enforcement, specially given the paragraph previous specification to 'have no obligation to remove such Co-Branding Logo'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, mPhase shall immediately cease use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, and shall make commercially reasonable efforts to remove such Co-Branding Logo from any Goods sold prior to the date of such termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that mPhase had 'no obligation' to remove the Co-Branding Logo from goods sold prior to termination. The changed text introduces an obligation, albeit a 'commercially reasonable' one, to remove the logo. This directly contradicts the previous 'no obligation' clause, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text as the contradiction exists between the original text and the changed text. This falls under the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as the enforcement of the 'commercially reasonable efforts' becomes unclear given the original explicit statement of 'no obligation'.
